rulings.Case Title: LAW OFFICES OF JON-MARK DOBRIN APC VS. AMJAD YOUSUF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Entry Of Defaut Against Amjad Yousuf; To Strike Purported "Notice" Requesting Stay As A Sham Pleading, Or As Containin False, Improper, Or Irrelevant Matter; Or Not Having Been Drawn In Conformity With The Laws Of This State [Ccp 435, Et Seq], For Relief From Any "Automatic Stay", For Order Of Court Determining That Deft Has Waived Right To Arbitrate Attorney Fee Dispute Pursuant To Business & Professions Code Section 6201 (B)
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 30, 2007, LINE 13. PLAINTIFF LAW OFFICES OF JON-MARK DOBRIN, APC'S Motion For Entry Of Default Against Amjad Yousuf; To Strike Purported "Notice" Requesting Stay As A Sham Pleading, Or As Containing False, Improper, Or Irrelevant Matter; Or Not Having Been Drawn In Conformity With The Laws Of This State [Ccp 435, Et Seq], For Relief From Any "Automatic Stay", For Order Of Court Determining That Defendantt Has Waived Right To Arbitrate Attorney Fee Dispute Pursuant To Business & Professions Code Section 6201 (B): THE FOLLOWING MOTIONS ARE DENIED: MOTION TO STRIKE, FOR RELIEF FROM STAY AND FOR ENTRY OF DEFAULT. DEFENDANT ONLY RECEIVED NOTICE OF THE RIGHT TO ARBITRATE, NOT UNDER THE MFAA. NOTICE MUST BE GIVEN OF THE THE RIGHT TO ARBITRATE UNDER THE MFAA BECAUSE IT IS A "SEPARATE AND DISTINCT ARBITRATION SCHEME." DEFENDANT HAS NOT WAIVED HIS RIGHTS UNDER THE MFAA. =(302/PJM/MW-X)

rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA HEALTHCARE ASSOCIATION,ON BEHALF OF ITS VS. BONTA, DIANA, DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA DEPARTMEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 30, 2007, LINE 14. RESPONDENTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF HEALTH SERVICES AND SANDRA SHEWRY, DIRECTOR OF THE CALIFORNIA MOTION For Judgment On The Pleadings: The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied. In ruling on the motion, the Court construes the allegations liberally to determine if there is a basis for liability. The complaint is replete with allegations that the State's methodology is "unsupported, arbitrary and unreasonable." See paragraphs 2, 3, 5 and 22. The complaint also pleads facts that, if proven, would support such allegations. See paragraphs 31-35. Plaintiff also pleads a beneficial interest in paragraphs 38 and 39. Waste Management of Alameda County, Inc. v. County of Alameda (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 1223, 1233. "Although traditional mandamus will not lie to control discretion of a public official or agency, that is, to force the exercise of discretion in a particular manner, it will lie to correct abuses of discretion, and will lie to force a particular action by the officer, when the law clearly establishes the petitioner's right to such action." Miller Family Home, Inc. v. Department of Social Services (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 488, 491. The absence of an enforceable right under section 1983 does not preclude relief under CCP section 1085. California Homeless & Housing Coalition v. Anderson (1995) 31 Cal.App.4th 450, 458. =(302/PJM/PB)
